title: Structure capture — close the collection-schema gap. Makes the snapshot carry the content-model STRUCTURE (the user-collection table DDL + PRIMARY KEYs + sequences + the directus_collections registration + the public-policy directus_permissions read grant) alongside the rows, captured atomically from the same sanctioned --dsn. stacksnap now auto-provisions a bootstrapped-gap stack before the row replay, so the directus replay EXITS 0 and a booted Directus serves a captured public simulation ANONYMOUSLY over HTTP — closing the M10 collection-schema gap at the tooling level.
position_note: M21 is the FIRST milestone of v1.5 'prop room' (M21->M25). It is the foundation the rest builds on: M21 makes the replay succeed, M22 boots the per-stack Directus as a compose service, M23 points services at it + closes referential gaps, M24 tells the corpus-wide truth, M25 proves it live. Strictly sequential. After this close: M22 (/developer-kit:build-milestone, section shape).
kind: an iterative milestone — production code in the SEPARATE gitignored rosetta-extensions authoring copy (stack-snapshot Go module: capture, cmd/stacksnap, directus, firewall, manifest, pg), milestone tracking + the net-new corpus/ops/directus-local.md in rosetta. The rosetta m21/structure-capture branch carries ONLY plan/doc/ledger records + corpus/ops/directus-local.md (net-new) + the snapshot-spec store-fork update + the README index row + the db-query SKILL re-verify-prod-schema doc; ALL Go is ext-repo-side (tag prop-room-m21).

gate.target: on a scratch offset Postgres + a bootstrapped directus/directus:11.6.1: stacksnap applies the captured structure -> `stacksnap replay --surface directus` exits 0 -> a booted Directus serves a captured public simulation over HTTP to an ANONYMOUS reader (GET /items/simulations?limit=1 -> 200 with a real row)
gate.achieved: MET by tooling (iter-08) — stacksnap captures the structure (DDL+PKs+seqs) + the serve rows (directus_collections registration + public-policy directus_permissions) behind the new firewall structural-metadata admissibility class, auto-provisions a bootstrapped-gap stack, and a booted+provisioned stack serves the captured catalog anonymously with NO hand SQL. Full capture->apply Go path live-validated; the literal HTTP boot+serve was DEMONSTRATED in iter-05 against the real 26-collection prod structure, and iter-08 rides that proven path.

iters.tok_flavors: 1 bootstrap tok (iter-01: authored TOK-01 staged-pipeline strategy + the 6-stage metric); 0 triggered toks (no 3-consecutive-no-progress trigger — every tik advanced the staged pipeline)
iters.orphans: none — every iter-NN has a closed progress.md; 1:1 commit map (one iter commit per tik + the bootstrap-tok commit + interleaved docs commits); the running ledger in progress.md has an entry per closed iter
iters.stage_trajectory: static baseline 2/6 -> iter-02 live baseline 2 -> iter-04 4 (Option A: 26-collection structure converges digest 6cd35278, replay exit 0) -> iter-05 6 DEMONSTRATED (anonymous serve, PRIMARY-KEY finding) -> iter-06/07/08 code-ification (capture core -> apply side -> serve half) = GATE MET BY TOOLING
iters.strategy_of_record: TOK-01 (staged-pipeline) refined by M21-D7 (digest-keying option A — capture all 26 collections + pin the Directus version, operator decision) + M21-D9 (the serve recipe + PRIMARY-KEY finding) + M21-D13 (the firewall structural-metadata admissibility class + the serve-row capture/apply)

tests.language: go (the stack-snapshot module — 12 packages). The rosetta-side milestone branch carries no Go; its deliverables are docs (corpus/ops/directus-local.md net-new + snapshot-spec update + README index row).
tests.go_delta_note: +59 stack-snapshot test+fuzz funcs vs the v1.3b/M20 close baseline (stack-snapshot 231 -> 290). The whole module total across all 4 Go modules: alignment 43 + clerkenstein 210 + stack-seeding 252 + stack-snapshot 290 = 795 (clerkenstein/stack-seeding unchanged by M21). The +59 = the structure-capture core (iter-06) + auto-provision (iter-07) + the serve half (iter-08) + the two final harden passes (21 new tests across firewall/manifest/capture/directus/cmd-stacksnap dimensions).
tests.coverage_note: directus + firewall at 100% stmts (the structure-capture + the carve-out fully exercised). manifest 98.4% (only the unreachable json.MarshalIndent-error defensive branch left). capture 98.9% (remaining branches non-M21). cmd/stacksnap 80.1% + pg 47.0% — the residual is EXCLUSIVELY the live-DB connection wrappers (pg.Connect/Close/CopyOut/CopyIn/Exec/ExecScript/QueryRow*) + the replayCmd/replayAdapter real-conn pass-throughs = the route-forward AP-1 conn-seam item (integration-only by nature, no conn seam). Reachable surface saturated.
tests.test_count_method: go test ./... -count=1 (12/12 packages ok); per-package -cover. 0 failures, 0 skips across the module. Test+Fuzz func count via grep of ^func (Test|Fuzz) across *_test.go (sorted unique).
tests.flake_gate: 5/5 clean, sequential, -shuffle=on, full module. Deterministic — no FAIL on any run. (The two final harden passes also recorded a 3-consecutive-clean run of the 21 new tests.)

coverage.note: The hardening-ledger.md final-mode sweep ran 2 passes (cumulative scope across all 6 M21-touched packages). Pass 1 took directus 94.2% -> 100% + cmd/stacksnap 79.5% -> 80.1%; Pass 2 took manifest 95.2% -> 98.4% + deepened firewall (identity/ordering guards) + capture (the CapturesStructure capability-mismatch branch). Stop condition: stabilized — every inline-reachable statement across the 6 packages is covered; the cross-pass delta on the reachable surface is < 2% (saturated). 21 new harden tests, 0 bugs surfaced (the M21 code held under error-path + edge-case enumeration).
coverage.seams_pinned: the firewall structural-metadata carve-out (admit directus_* system tables iff zero tenant-scope columns; whole-column-not-substring match; extend-never-loosen proven independent of the row-side AssertPlan/AssertCaptured gates), the assert-then-read ordering (admissibility BEFORE any serve-row read), the auto-provision gap-guard (provisions ONLY a true bootstrap gap, no-op on a diverged target -> clean exit 5 not raw exit 1), the redefined exit semantics (empty->4, gap+no-structure->5, diverged->5, gap+structure->0, stable+distinct), the default-reference sequence capture (robust to non-owned sequences), the privilege-visibility alignment (capture intersects pg_catalog with information_schema so the table set matches the digest's view), the manifest additive Structure artifact (Marshal->Parse->re-Validate round-trip), the multi-snapshot tie-break determinism (findStructureSnapshot selects the first store.List() structure-bearing ref — deterministic on LocalFS.List()'s sort, pinned against an order-controlled fake).

prod_safety_invariant_verification.verdict: GREEN — structure capture stays a read-only / bounded / operator-confirmed / public-only prod READ behind the firewall, now EXTENDED (not loosened) to admit structural metadata.
prod_safety_invariant_verification.structural_metadata_carve_out: firewall.AssertStructuralMetadata admits a directus_* system table as 'structure, not tenant data' ONLY if it carries NONE of TenantScopeColumns (organization_id/tenant_id/private/user/owner/user_created/user_updated). directus_collections + directus_permissions carry zero -> admissible (verified: sanctioned prod read + the live fixture). directus_access EXCLUDED (has a `user` uuid column); directus_policies NOT captured (both bootstrap-provided). Pinned by firewall/structural_metadata_harden_test.go (each identity column individually rejected + named, real serve-table column sets admitted, whole-column match semantics, extend-never-loosen: the carve-out cannot launder a row-side AssertPlan violation — predicate-agnostic).
prod_safety_invariant_verification.assert_then_read: the carve-out runs admissibility on the introspected column set BEFORE any serve-row is materialized, so an unexpected tenant column aborts before the read. Pinned by structure_harden_test.go (admissibility-fail skips render) + serve_test.go (the ordering fake asserts the admissibility probe ran before the serve-row reads).
prod_safety_invariant_verification.operator_gated_prod_read: the real structure source (prod-faithful column types + the registry rows) exists only in the prod directus schema (self-contained options exhausted, M21-D6); the read was OPERATOR-SANCTIONED (bounded, read-only, public-only, via the wired postgres MCP for the structural read — M21-D3 in the operator-memory; recorded in MEMORY.md m21-prod-structural-read-sanctioned). All capture stays behind the M9a capture-source policy + AssertPublicOnly.
prod_safety_invariant_verification.dropped_dump_reader_stays_gone: the dropped pg_dump-file-reader (M9b-D9) did NOT resurrect — TestDroppedDumpFlagStaysGone pins it gone (re-confirmed at close).
prod_safety_invariant_verification.writes_only_per_stack_isolated: the apply path (ExecScript of the structure artifact) targets the per-stack-isolated offset directus schema; auto-provision is gated on a true bootstrap GAP so it never mutates a diverged or prod target.

delivered.directus_local_md: corpus/ops/directus-local.md (net-new, dual-level + code-cited): the per-stack local Directus spec — the 11.6.1 bootstrap empirics (bootstrap creates only the 27 system tables, DB_SEARCH_PATH=directus, node cli.js bootstrap, the admin@<stack>.example.com email-validator fix #M21-D1, split host/container DSNs), the structure-capture model (all-26-collection DDL + PRIMARY KEYs #M21-D9 + default-reference sequences #M21-D11 + serve rows #M21-D13, scoped to the digest's information_schema view #M21-D10), the redefined stacksnap exit codes (empty->4 / gap+structure->0 / gap-no-structure & diverged->5 #M21-D12), the version-skew rule (option A: capture all 26 + pin 11.6.1 since its system digest b4cb55bc == prod's system-only digest, no skew #M21-D7/D8 + warn-on-mismatch), and the firewall structural-metadata admissibility carve-out #M21-D13. Indexed in the corpus/ops/README index table; cross-linked from snapshot-spec/snapshot-cold-start/safety. Explicitly scopes the lifecycle half (compose service + cutover) to M22/M23.
delivered.snapshot_spec_update: corpus/ops/snapshot-spec.md § the per-stack Directus store fork — step 2 (was 'NOT YET AUTOMATED — the M10 collection-schema gap') updated to 'closed at the tooling level by M21' with the apply-structure mechanism + a cross-ref to directus-local.md, kept honest that EXECUTION at bring-up is still M22 (print-only today).
delivered.readme_index_row: corpus/ops/README.md — added the Local Directus index row (the recurring index-row-miss class the v1.5/M24 README-index guard targets — caught + landed here at close).
delivered.structure_capture_extension: rosetta-extensions stack-snapshot (tag prop-room-m21): directus.CaptureStructure (the dynamic digest-aligned DDL+PK+seq+serve-row generator over *pg.Conn) + the manifest additive Structure artifact + firewall.AssertStructuralMetadata + cmd/stacksnap tryAutoProvision (apply structure on a bootstrap-gap cache miss before the row replay) + the redefined exit classifier. Ext HEAD 835d940 (final harden); the code halves: iter-06 capture core (2c42ed5) -> iter-07 apply side (fe01d64) -> iter-08 serve half + final harden.
delivered.exit_codes_redefined: stacksnap exit-4 semantics: was 'schema missing -> a capture can't help'; now empty->4 (provision the stack first), gap+structure->auto-provision then exit 0, gap+no-structure->5, diverged->5. The structure artifact IS what provisions the schema.

review.findings_note: 5 findings: 1 scope (the unwritten committed deliverable corpus/ops/directus-local.md -> Fate-1 authored), 1 should-fix code-quality (an error-label consistency nit at directus/structure.go:364 -> fixed, retested green), 1 docs (the snapshot-spec store-fork update + the README index row), 0 adversarial-new (the 5 simulated scenarios — the firewall carve-out under a future identity column, the auto-provision partial-apply, serve-row name injection, the statement-count provenance, the concurrent structure+row capture — were ALL already handled + test-pinned), 1 fate-routing (directus_files ref capture -> Fate-3 annotated to M23).
review.scope: GREEN after Phase 7 — the iterative gate is MET (closed-on-gate); all 8 iters closed with a 1:1 commit map, no orphans, 0 TODO/FIXME in the M21-touched production code. review.code_quality: GREEN — the cross-cutting review (parallel Explore agent over all 6 packages) found consistent error-wrapping with stage-named errors.Is-unwrappable errors, no dead code beyond the documented-and-carried media.go (directus_files still dead — expected, routed to M23), assert-then-read boundary safety, proper pgxpool Acquire/defer-Release resource hygiene, clean module boundaries. 1 should-fix (the serve-row admissibility error label) fixed. go build + go vet CLEAN.
review.docs: the net-new corpus/ops/directus-local.md (scope deliverable, authored at close) + the snapshot-spec store-fork honesty update + the README index row. All outbound + self-anchor links resolve. The M21 decisions that a future developer needs (bootstrap empirics, PK finding, version-skew, firewall carve-out, exit codes) are blended into directus-local.md via (#M21-DK) ref-tags.
review.tests: GREEN — full stack-snapshot suite 12/12 packages, 290 test+fuzz funcs, 0 failures / 0 skips; the M21-touched-package coverage matches the hardening-ledger (directus/firewall 100%, manifest 98.4%, capture 98.9%, cmd/stacksnap 80.1%, pg 47.0% live-DB residual). Post-fix re-run + flake gate 5/5 shuffled GREEN.
review.adversarial: Phase 2c — 5 scenarios (firewall carve-out vs a future un-listed identity column [conservative human-reviewed list + per-column tests]; auto-provision on a partially-provisioned target [gap-guard counts user collections, no-op on >0, mandatory re-probe, idempotent-by-collision design]; serve-row SQL injection via collection names [fixed package identifier list + fuzz-tested escaping]; statement-count provenance drift [comment-semicolon-skipping countStatements + test]; concurrent structure+row capture under a dropped collection [catalog-only capture reads the schema shape at call time — no row-staleness risk]) — ALL already handled + test-pinned. No new finding required a code change. Recorded in decisions.md M21-D13's general-lesson + the harden ledger's cross-iter findings.
review.decision_triage: M21-D1/D7/D8/D9/D10/D11/D12/D13 (the user-facing empirics + model) blended into corpus/ops/directus-local.md with (#M21-DK) ref-tags; the maintainer-only reasoning trails (the digest-keying option-A-vs-B analysis M21-D5, the structure-source exhaustion investigation M21-D6, the mechanism-validation M21-D2) stay in decisions.md as archive.
review.deferral_audit: GREEN — knowledge/plan/releases/01.50-prop-room/m21-structure-capture/audit-deferrals/deferral-audit-2026-06-13-m21-close.md. M21 is the FIRST milestone of v1.5 -> nothing inherited. 5 items in scope, all single (0 repeat, 0 chronic, 0 aged-out): DEF-M21-01 (replayCmd conn-seam) -> tracked follow-up iter; DEF-M21-02 (serve-live-integration harness) -> live-integration backlog; DEF-M21-03 (directus_files ref capture) -> Fate-3 ANNOTATED to M23; DEF-M21-04 (the 20 dangling relations) -> Fate-2 already owned by M23; DEF-M10-01 (S3 blob bytes) -> keep-deferred backlog (re-signed fresh at v1.5 design, orthogonal to M21, not aged). 0 blockers.

notes: M21 is the FIRST + foundational milestone of v1.5 'prop room' — it closes the M10 collection-schema gap that made every stack read public content live from prod. The iterative shape was the honest call: Directus's anonymous-read permissions + the registry-row firewall carve-out + the cache-digest convergence were undesigned territory that only broke live (the load-bearing PRIMARY-KEY finding — Directus silently 403s a PK-less collection even for admin, while the digest still converges — is exactly the kind of empirical quirk a fixed checklist would have missed). 8 iters (1 bootstrap tok + 7 tiks), 0 triggered toks — every tik advanced the 6-stage pipeline (static 2 -> live 2 -> 4 -> 6 demonstrated -> code-ified met). Gate MET by tooling: stacksnap captures the structure (DDL+PKs+seqs+serve rows) behind the new firewall structural-metadata admissibility class, auto-provisions a bootstrap-gap stack, and serves the captured catalog anonymously with NO hand SQL. Two final harden passes (cumulative-scope) took the 6 packages to a saturated reachable surface (directus/firewall 100%, manifest 98.4%, capture 98.9%), +21 tests, 0 bugs. Close was clean: 1 scope (the committed-but-unwritten corpus/ops/directus-local.md -> Fate-1 authored, the structure half; M22 adds the lifecycle half to the same shared doc), 1 should-fix code (an error-label nit), 1 docs (snapshot-spec honesty + the README index row), 0 adversarial-new (5 scenarios all already pinned), the deferral audit GREEN (0 repeat / 0 aged; directus_files -> Fate-3 M23, the 20 dangling relations -> Fate-2 M23). prod-safety GREEN: structure capture stays a read-only/bounded/operator-confirmed/public-only prod read behind the firewall, EXTENDED to admit structural metadata (zero tenant-scope columns) without loosening it; assert-then-read; the dropped dump-reader stays gone. Go stack-snapshot 231 -> 290 (+59); flake 0 (5/5 shuffled). Next: M22 (/developer-kit:build-milestone — executed provisioning + the per-stack Directus compose-service lifecycle).
